title: Senior Data Scientist
description:
Acme Corp is looking for a Senior Data Scientist to join our growing Analytics and AI team in Vancouver.

Responsibilities:
- Design, build, and deploy production-grade ML models for customer churn prediction and lifetime value estimation
- Own the full model lifecycle from problem framing and feature engineering through deployment and monitoring
- Partner with product and engineering to define success metrics and experiment designs
- Run A/B tests and interpret results using causal inference methods
- Mentor two to three junior data scientists on the team
- Present model findings and business recommendations to senior stakeholders quarterly

Requirements:
- 5+ years of experience in applied data science
- Proficiency in Python, scikit-learn, XGBoost, and SQL
- Familiarity with cloud ML platforms (AWS SageMaker or GCP Vertex AI)
- Strong communication skills; comfortable presenting to non-technical audiences

Salary: $130,000 – $155,000 CAD annually, plus equity and benefits.

title: Data Scientist, Senior
description:
We are hiring a Data Scientist, Senior level to support our Analytics and AI organization in Vancouver. This role is identical to our LinkedIn posting but is listed separately for Indeed applicants.

Key responsibilities:
- Build churn and LTV prediction models in Python
- Manage end-to-end ML model lifecycle including deployment to SageMaker
- Design and analyse A/B experiments using causal inference
- Mentor junior team members
- Communicate findings to leadership

Compensation range: 130K to 155K CAD.

This is a duplicate of our LinkedIn posting — intentionally included here to test deduplication logic.

title: ML Engineer
description:
Watershed AI builds climate risk analytics products powered by ML. We are seeking an ML Engineer to join our core platform team in Vancouver.

What you will do:
- Design and implement real-time inference pipelines for our climate risk scoring models
- Develop and maintain MLflow experiment tracking and model registry workflows
- Build feature stores using Feast and optimize feature freshness for low-latency serving
- Write production-quality Python services using FastAPI
- Collaborate with research scientists to translate prototype models into scalable inference code
- Instrument models with monitoring dashboards (data drift, prediction drift) using Evidently AI
- Participate in on-call rotation for ML infrastructure

Requirements:
- 3+ years ML engineering experience
- Strong Python skills; Kubernetes and Docker experience required
- Familiarity with transformer model serving (TorchServe or Triton)
- Experience with distributed training on GPU clusters

Salary: $140,000 – $165,000 CAD plus options.

title: Senior ML Engineer — Recommendations
description:
Shopify's Buyer ML team is looking for a Senior ML Engineer to lead recommendations and personalization infrastructure. This is a fully remote role open to Canadian residents.

Responsibilities:
- Architect and own the end-to-end recommendations ML platform (candidate retrieval, ranking, serving)
- Lead migration from batch offline models to real-time two-tower retrieval at Shopify scale (billions of events/day)
- Partner with applied scientists to productionize embedding models
- Drive technical design reviews and mentor engineers across teams
- Define SLAs for inference latency and uptime and ensure systems meet them

Requirements:
- 5+ years building production ML systems
- Expert-level Python and proficiency in Go or Java for systems work
- Experience with large-scale retrieval systems (Faiss, ScaNN, or similar)
- Proven track record of shipping high-traffic ML features

Compensation: $160,000 – $190,000 CAD.

title: Data Engineer
description:
Hootsuite is searching for a Data Engineer to join the Data Platform team in Vancouver. You will be responsible for building and maintaining the data infrastructure that powers analytics, ML features, and executive dashboards across the company.

Responsibilities:
- Design and build ELT pipelines using dbt and Airflow on GCP BigQuery
- Own data quality monitoring and alerting across 50+ critical data assets
- Migrate legacy ETL scripts from Talend to a modern dbt-based architecture
- Implement column-level data lineage documentation in dbt
- Work with analytics engineers and data scientists to model raw event data into reliable business metrics
- Maintain Terraform infrastructure for BigQuery, Cloud Composer, and GCS

Requirements:
- 3+ years data engineering experience
- Expert SQL and Python
- Hands-on dbt and Airflow experience
- GCP or AWS data platform experience required

Salary: $110,000 – $130,000 CAD.

title: Senior Data Engineer
description:
A stealth-mode fintech startup backed by Tier 1 VCs is seeking a Senior Data Engineer to build their data foundation from the ground up. The role is fully remote and open to Canadian residents.

What you will build:
- Real-time ingestion pipelines from core banking APIs into a Lakehouse (Apache Iceberg on S3)
- Medallion architecture (bronze / silver / gold) using Apache Spark on Databricks
- Streaming pipelines with Apache Kafka for payment event processing
- Data contracts using Great Expectations to enforce schema and quality SLAs
- A self-serve analytics layer on top of the gold tier using dbt and Metabase

Requirements:
- 4+ years data engineering experience
- Strong Spark, Kafka, and SQL skills
- Experience building Lakehouse architectures
- Familiarity with financial data regulatory requirements (PCI-DSS, SOC 2) is a plus

Salary not disclosed — competitive based on experience.

title: Data Analyst — Growth
description:
Trulioo, the global identity verification leader, is hiring a Data Analyst focused on Growth to join our Revenue Operations team in Vancouver.

Responsibilities:
- Build and maintain funnel dashboards in Looker covering acquisition, activation, and retention metrics
- Partner with Growth PMs and Marketing to define and track experiment KPIs
- Write complex SQL queries against Snowflake to extract insights from user journey events
- Conduct cohort analyses and attribution modelling for paid and organic channels
- Automate recurring reporting for the executive team using Looker scheduled looks
- Present findings in weekly business review meetings

Requirements:
- 2+ years data analyst or business analyst experience
- Expert SQL; Looker or Tableau experience
- Comfort with Excel / Google Sheets for ad-hoc analysis
- Basic Python for data wrangling is a plus

Salary: $80,000 – $95,000 CAD.

title: Senior Data Analyst
description:
TELUS Health is transforming how Canadians access health care through technology. We are seeking a Senior Data Analyst to support our health outcomes analytics team in Vancouver.

What you will do:
- Design and execute analyses of patient outcomes data across millions of anonymized records
- Build predictive models in Python to identify high-risk populations for preventive interventions (regression and decision-tree based — not deep learning)
- Partner with clinicians and health economists to translate analytical findings into clinical practice guidelines
- Own recurring reporting for government payer clients using Tableau
- Ensure all work complies with PIPEDA and provincial health privacy legislation

Requirements:
- 4+ years health analytics or similar domain experience
- Expert SQL; Python for data analysis
- Tableau or Power BI experience
- Familiarity with health data standards (HL7, FHIR) is a strong asset

Salary range not listed — see HR for details.

title: Applied Data Scientist — NLP
description:
Coveo powers intelligent search and recommendations experiences for enterprise clients. We are looking for an Applied Data Scientist with a focus on NLP to join our AI Research team.

Responsibilities:
- Fine-tune and evaluate large language models (LLaMA 3, GPT-4, Mistral) for enterprise search relevance tasks
- Build retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines combining dense retrieval (bi-encoder / cross-encoder) with generative reranking
- Design and run offline evaluation suites using NDCG, MRR, and human judgment datasets
- Partner with product teams to ship LLM-powered features to Coveo's SaaS platform
- Contribute to applied NLP research — occasional publications and patent applications encouraged
- Stay current with literature (NeurIPS, EMNLP, ACL) and evaluate applicability of new techniques to Coveo's use cases

Requirements:
- PhD or Master's in NLP, CS, or related field, or equivalent industry experience
- Strong Python; PyTorch and HuggingFace experience required
- Experience with vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector)

Salary: $125,000 – $150,000 CAD.

title: Principal Data Scientist
description:
Loblaws Digital is building the AI capabilities that power Canada's largest grocery retailer. We are seeking a Principal Data Scientist to lead our Demand Forecasting and Pricing Science function.

Responsibilities:
- Define the multi-year technical roadmap for demand forecasting and price elasticity modelling across 1,100+ retail locations
- Lead a team of 5 data scientists and be accountable for model quality, production reliability, and business impact
- Represent Data Science in executive-level product planning and OKR definition
- Drive architectural decisions for the ML platform (feature stores, model registry, deployment strategy)
- Collaborate with operations, supply chain, and merchandising leadership to align science priorities with business objectives
- Recruit and mentor senior ICs; define levelling standards for the Data Science function

Requirements:
- 10+ years applied data science; 3+ years leading teams
- Deep expertise in time-series forecasting (Prophet, DeepAR, TFT)
- Strong communication skills; experience presenting to C-suite

Salary: $190,000 – $220,000 CAD plus significant equity.

title: Data Scientist
description:
Klue is a competitive intelligence platform used by enterprise sales teams. We are hiring a Data Scientist to work on our content intelligence and signal extraction pipeline.

Responsibilities:
- Build and iterate on NLP models to extract competitive signals from unstructured web content (news, job postings, product release notes, review sites)
- Design evaluation frameworks for text classification, named entity recognition, and information extraction tasks
- Own data annotation pipelines using Label Studio and prodigy
- Collaborate with product and engineering to integrate model outputs into Klue's core platform
- Run experiments to improve precision and recall on competitive signal detection
- Work with a small team — expect to contribute to data ingestion and feature engineering as well as modelling

Requirements:
- 2+ years NLP or applied ML experience
- Python, HuggingFace Transformers, spaCy
- Familiarity with information extraction or document understanding tasks

Salary not listed — competitive for Vancouver market.

title: ML Engineer — Computer Vision
description:
D-Wave is hiring an ML Engineer with a focus on computer vision to support our quantum-classical hybrid research applications.

Responsibilities:
- Build and deploy computer vision models for defect detection in manufacturing imagery (classification and object detection)
- Integrate PyTorch models into a FastAPI inference service containerized with Docker and deployed to AWS ECS
- Implement automated retraining pipelines triggered by data drift detection
- Collaborate with quantum computing researchers to explore hybrid quantum-classical optimisation approaches for CV tasks
- Maintain the MLflow experiment registry and model versioning for CV projects
- Contribute to internal tooling for image annotation and data quality review

Requirements:
- 3+ years ML engineering with CV focus
- PyTorch, torchvision, ONNX export experience
- Docker and AWS experience required

Salary: $120,000 – $145,000 CAD.

title: Data Scientist — Business Intelligence
description:
Absolute Software is looking for a Data Scientist to support our Business Intelligence and Revenue Operations team. This role is primarily focused on executive reporting, KPI dashboards, and business analytics — not on building ML models.

Responsibilities:
- Design and own executive dashboards in Tableau covering ARR, NRR, churn, and pipeline coverage
- Build and maintain SQL-based reporting on Snowflake for Finance, Sales, and Customer Success
- Conduct ad-hoc analyses to answer business questions from leadership (Why did churn spike in Q3? Which segments are underperforming?)
- Partner with Finance on forecasting using spreadsheet-based models
- Automate recurring board-level reporting packs using Python and Pandas for data preparation

Note: This role is titled 'Data Scientist' internally but involves minimal to no ML modelling. Ideal for someone with strong analytical skills who is comfortable owning reporting infrastructure.

Salary: $90,000 – $105,000 CAD.

title: Senior Data Analyst — Product Analytics
description:
CREA (Canadian Real Estate Association), operator of Realtor.ca, is seeking a Senior Data Analyst focused on Product Analytics to join our remote-first data team.

Responsibilities:
- Own product analytics for Realtor.ca's consumer search and listing surfaces, serving 10M+ monthly visitors
- Design and analyse A/B experiments using a Python-based experimentation framework
- Build funnel and engagement dashboards in Looker Studio to support product decisions
- Partner with product managers to define metrics frameworks and OKRs for new product features
- Develop self-serve analytics resources and documentation for non-technical stakeholders
- Investigate anomalies in search and listing engagement data and present root-cause analyses

Requirements:
- 4+ years in product analytics or equivalent role
- Expert SQL (BigQuery preferred); Python for analysis and automation
- Experience designing and interpreting A/B experiments
- Looker, Looker Studio, or Tableau proficiency

Salary: $100,000 – $120,000 CAD.

title: Data Engineer — ML Platform
description:
Bench Accounting is building the ML Platform that powers AI-assisted bookkeeping for small business owners across North America. We are looking for a Data Engineer to own the data infrastructure that feeds our ML models.

Responsibilities:
- Build and maintain Fivetran and custom ingestion connectors for 20+ SaaS data sources (QuickBooks, Stripe, Plaid, bank APIs)
- Implement dbt models for the feature engineering layer that feeds our bookkeeping classification models
- Develop and monitor Airflow DAGs for daily batch ML feature pipelines
- Instrument data quality checks using Great Expectations across all ML-serving datasets
- Own data contracts between the ML platform and the product engineering team
- Collaborate with data scientists to optimize data availability for model training and evaluation

Requirements:
- 3+ years data engineering experience
- Strong Python and SQL; dbt and Airflow experience required
- Exposure to ML feature engineering is strongly preferred
- Experience with financial data is a plus

Salary: $105,000 – $125,000 CAD.

title: Senior Data Scientist — NLP
description:
Clio is the world's leading legal technology company. We are seeking a Senior Data Scientist with NLP expertise to join our AI research team.

Responsibilities:
- Fine-tune large language models (GPT-4, LLaMA) for legal document analysis and contract review tasks
- Build retrieval-augmented generation pipelines using dense bi-encoder retrieval over legal corpora
- Design and run offline evaluation suites using NDCG and human-annotated legal datasets
- Research transformer architectures for long-document understanding (contract clauses, legal briefs)
- Work closely with product teams to ship NLP features into Clio's practice management SaaS platform
- Publish internal research notes and contribute to external venues (ACL, EMNLP)

Requirements:
- PhD or Master's in NLP or equivalent industry experience with transformers
- PyTorch, HuggingFace Transformers; experience with RAG systems
- Familiarity with legal or regulatory text is a strong asset

Salary: $130,000 – $155,000 CAD.

title: Senior Data Scientist — Computer Vision
description:
Clio Technologies is expanding its AI capabilities. We are looking for a Senior Data Scientist focused on Computer Vision to support document digitization and intelligent optical character recognition for legal filings.

Responsibilities:
- Design and train object detection and segmentation models to extract structure from scanned legal documents
- Build automated OCR and layout analysis pipelines using PyTorch and Detectron2
- Develop active learning workflows to iteratively improve annotation quality on court filing images
- Integrate vision model inference into Clio's document processing microservices using TorchServe
- Collaborate with the product team to define model quality thresholds for production deployment
- Run ablation studies to measure the trade-off between inference speed and accuracy for on-device OCR

Requirements:
- 4+ years ML experience with a CV focus; proficient in PyTorch and torchvision
- Experience with OCR toolkits (Tesseract, PaddleOCR) and document layout analysis
- Docker and Kubernetes experience for inference pipeline deployment

Salary: $125,000 – $150,000 CAD.
